title: In a Still Turbulent Strait of Hormuz, Stranded Ships Wait for Clarity
summary: Shipping companies had hoped to get their stranded vessels out, but faced uncertainty as violence flared again in the region.

title: Firm Tied to Trump Donor Got No-Bid Contract to Clean Reflecting Pool

summary: With midterms approaching and negotiations heating up, some lawmakers are apprehensive over where the discussions are headed.

title: Who Is Andy Burnham, the Man Who Could Be Britain’s Next Prime Minister?
summary: Charismatic, northern and exuding a relaxed optimism, Mr. Burnham is a contrast to Keir Starmer. His allies hope he could mend Labour’s relationship with voters.

summary: The drone attack that sent plumes of smoke rising over Moscow intensified Ukrainian hopes of bringing the war to Russia.

summary: Ukrainian drone attacks have achieved breakthroughs, but only ballistic missiles can push Russia to reconsider its war objectives, military experts and officials say.

summary: As a new, potentially record-breaking El Niño begins, researchers are vigorously debating whether climate change is driving the phenomenon’s intensity.

summary: In “Regime Change,” two New York Times journalists offer a riveting chronicle of the weird fusion of reality and show business in the White House.

summary: Is the Iran war an early sign of “superpower suicide"?

title: Hannah Pingree and Bobby Charles Will Face Off in Maine Governor’s Race
summary: Mr. Charles, a conservative Republican, had led the field ahead of a ranked-choice runoff. But Ms. Pingree, a former Maine House speaker, had been in a tight race with other Democrats.

summary: In a high-profile Democratic House primary, there’s a fair chance that would-be voters may have been sweet-talked by the proud mothers of leading candidates.

summary: The case, which resulted in a short jail sentence despite years of alleged abuse, has drawn outcry in Texas. So have the attempts to politicize it.

title: San Francisco Film Patrons Are Found Dead on Side of Highway
summary: Judith Sheldon, 84, daughter of an Oscar-winning director, and her husband, Wylie, 86, were traveling to Oregon on a hot day. Their deaths are under investigation.

title: Trump Put His Own Interests Above All in the Iran Deal
summary: The Iran deal wasn’t about peace but answering Trump’s personal needs.

title: I Keep Telling People We’re Living in This Dystopian Novel
summary: The 2010 novel “Super Sad True Love Story” foreshadowed our current world. So I asked its author, Gary Shteyngart, how he sees the world today and how we might find pleasure in it.

title: Strikes in Moscow

title: Buildings May Soon Have ‘Immune Systems’ That Fight Airborne Disease
summary: Following the pandemic, the federal government is spending $150 million on new technology to ensure clean indoor air. Here’s what scientists are pursuing.
